gloss.en: the fact of owning something

words.example: Home ownership has grown in recent years.

gloss.en: a large male cow used for farm work

words.example: The farmer used an ox to pull the cart.

gloss.en: a gas in air that living things need to breathe

words.example: The patient was not getting enough oxygen.

gloss.en: a gas in the air that blocks harmful sun rays

words.example: The ozone layer protects us from the sun.

gloss.en: an informal word for father

words.example: My pa taught me how to fish.

gloss.en: the speed at which someone moves or works

words.example: We walked at a steady pace.

gloss.en: something wrapped up to be sent or given

words.example: A large package arrived for you today.

gloss.en: very full of people or things

words.example: The train was packed during rush hour.

gloss.en: a small paper or card container for goods

words.example: She bought a packet of biscuits.

gloss.en: a brush used for painting

words.example: He cleaned his paintbrush after work.

gloss.en: loose clothes worn for sleeping

words.example: She put on her pajamas before bed.

gloss.en: a flat wooden frame for moving goods

words.example: The boxes were stacked on a pallet.

gloss.en: a tall tropical tree with leaves at the top

words.example: A palm tree grew near the beach.

gloss.en: a thin flat cake fried in a pan

words.example: I had a pancake for breakfast.

gloss.en: relating to a disease spread over a wide area

words.example: The pandemic flu spread across many countries.

gloss.en: a flat piece forming part of a door or wall

words.example: One glass panel in the door was cracked.

gloss.en: a sudden strong feeling of fear

words.example: There was a moment of panic in the crowd.

gloss.en: a sweet tropical fruit with orange flesh

words.example: We ate fresh papaya for dessert.

gloss.en: using computers instead of paper

words.example: The office is going paperless this year.

gloss.en: written work like forms and documents

words.example: I have a lot of paperwork to finish.

gloss.en: a public celebration with people marching

words.example: We watched the parade through the streets.

gloss.en: a statement that seems to contradict itself

words.example: It is a paradox that he loves quiet but lives in a city.

gloss.en: lines that stay the same distance apart

words.example: The two roads run parallel to each other.

gloss.en: loss of the ability to move part of the body

words.example: The illness caused paralysis in his legs.

gloss.en: a person trained to give emergency medical help

words.example: A paramedic treated the injured driver.

gloss.en: a soldier trained to jump from planes

words.example: The paratrooper landed safely in the field.

gloss.en: the elected group that makes a country's laws

words.example: The new law was passed by parliament.

gloss.en: working only part of a day or week

words.example: She is looking for a part-time job.

gloss.en: a person taking part in an activity

words.example: Each participant in the study was an adult.

gloss.en: the act of taking part in something

words.example: The show needs lots of audience participation.

gloss.en: a very small piece of something

words.example: A particle of dust landed on the screen.

gloss.en: used to point to one specific thing

words.example: There is one particular patient I want you to see.

gloss.en: a strong supporter of a party or cause

words.example: He is a loyal partisan of the new leader.

gloss.en: to some extent; not completely

words.example: She stayed home partly because of the rain.

gloss.en: the state of being partners in business

words.example: They went into partnership together.

gloss.en: a narrow way connecting rooms or places

words.example: A dark narrow passage led to the hall.

gloss.en: showing very strong feelings

words.example: She is passionate about her work.

gloss.en: in a way that shows strong feelings

words.example: He spoke passionately about justice.

gloss.en: secret letters or numbers to access a system

words.example: Enter your username and password to log in.

gloss.en: relating to the countryside or to caring for people

words.example: The painting showed a calm pastoral scene.

gloss.en: the ability to stay calm and wait

words.example: She has little patience with lazy people.

gloss.en: able to wait calmly without getting angry

words.example: You will have to be patient and wait.

gloss.en: in a calm way without getting angry

words.example: She waited patiently for the bus.

gloss.en: a person who loves their country

words.example: He was a true patriot who served his country.

gloss.en: showing love for your country

words.example: They sang a patriotic song together.

gloss.en: love and loyalty for your country

words.example: His speech was full of patriotism.

gloss.en: a short stop in talking or doing something

words.example: There was a long pause before she answered.

gloss.en: to cover a road or area with a hard surface

words.example: They plan to pave the small road soon.

gloss.en: a hard path at the side of a road for walking

words.example: She walked along the pavement to school.

gloss.en: the foot of an animal such as a cat or dog

words.example: The cat hurt its paw on a sharp stone.

gloss.en: a person who lends money for left items

words.example: He sold his watch to the pawnbroker.

gloss.en: a shop that lends money for items left there

words.example: She took the ring to the pawnshop.

gloss.en: the act of paying or money that is paid

words.example: What method of payment do you prefer?

gloss.en: in a calm and quiet way

words.example: The baby slept peacefully all night.

gloss.en: a soft round fruit with sweet yellow flesh

words.example: I ate a ripe peach in the garden.

gloss.en: a small round white gem from an oyster

words.example: She wore a single pearl on a chain.

gloss.en: a small smooth stone

words.example: He threw a pebble into the lake.

gloss.en: a part you press with your foot to move something

words.example: She pressed the pedal to stop the car.

gloss.en: too concerned with small rules and details

words.example: He was pedantic about every small mistake.

gloss.en: a person who is walking, not in a vehicle

words.example: A pedestrian crossed the busy street.

gloss.en: to pass urine from the body

words.example: The dog needs to pee before bed.

gloss.en: the outer skin of a fruit or vegetable

words.example: Throw the orange peel in the bin.

gloss.en: a quick or secret look

words.example: She took a quick peep through the door.

gloss.en: a person of the same age or status as you

words.example: She enjoys the respect of her peers.

gloss.en: a clip or hook for holding or hanging things

words.example: Hang your coat on the peg by the door.

gloss.en: to punish someone for breaking a rule

words.example: Players who cheat will be penalised.

gloss.en: to punish someone for breaking a rule

words.example: The team was penalized for the foul.

gloss.en: a punishment for breaking a law or rule

words.example: The judge imposed a heavy penalty.

gloss.en: to go into or through something

words.example: The light could not penetrate the thick fog.

gloss.en: a black and white sea bird that cannot fly

words.example: A penguin slid across the ice.

gloss.en: a medicine that kills harmful bacteria

words.example: The doctor gave him penicillin for the infection.

gloss.en: money paid regularly to a retired person

words.example: He lives on a small pension now.

gloss.en: a flat shape with five straight sides

words.example: The students drew a pentagon in class.

gloss.en: to understand or see something in a certain way

words.example: Many perceive the change as a big step forward.

gloss.en: an amount expressed as a part of one hundred

words.example: What percentage of the class passed the test?

gloss.en: the way you see or understand something

words.example: The ad aimed to change public perception.

gloss.en: the state of lasting for a very long time

words.example: The agreement gave the deal a sense of permanence.

gloss.en: lasting for a long time or forever

words.example: She could not find a permanent job.

gloss.en: in a way that lasts forever

words.example: His arm was permanently damaged.

gloss.en: to spread through every part of something

words.example: The smell of coffee began to permeate the house.

gloss.en: to allow someone to do something

words.example: The rules do not permit smoking here.

gloss.en: the act of trying hard and not giving up

words.example: Her perseverance helped her pass the exam.

gloss.en: the act of continuing to try despite difficulty

words.example: His persistence finally won the deal.

gloss.en: giving human qualities to things or ideas

words.example: The poem uses personification of the wind.

gloss.en: to be a perfect example of a quality

words.example: She seems to personify kindness itself.

gloss.en: a particular way of thinking about something

words.example: Try to see it from a global perspective.

gloss.en: liquid that comes from the skin; sweat

words.example: Perspiration ran down his face in the heat.

gloss.en: the act of making someone believe or do something

words.example: After much persuasion, she agreed to go.

gloss.en: a person who expects bad things to happen

words.example: He is a pessimist who always fears the worst.

gloss.en: expecting bad things to happen

words.example: She felt pessimistic about the result.

gloss.en: extremely frightened

words.example: He was petrified of the dark room.

gloss.en: small and not important

words.example: They argued over petty details.

gloss.en: a measure of how acidic or basic something is

words.example: We tested the ph of the water.

gloss.en: a person trained to prepare and sell medicines

words.example: The pharmacist gave me the right medicine.

gloss.en: a stage in a process of change

words.example: We are in the final phase of the project.

gloss.en: a fact or event, especially an unusual one

words.example: Rainbows are a natural phenomenon.

gloss.en: the study of life, meaning and knowledge

words.example: She studies the philosophy of science.

gloss.en: a number used to measure circles, about 3.14

words.example: The value of pi is about 3.14.

gloss.en: an act of choosing something

words.example: Take your pick from the menu.

gloss.en: a vegetable kept in vinegar or salt water

words.example: She added a pickle to her sandwich.

gloss.en: hard to please; very careful in choosing

words.example: He is very picky about his food.

gloss.en: a painting, drawing or photo of something

words.example: He likes books with lots of pictures.

gloss.en: pretty and charming to look at

words.example: They stayed in a picturesque village.

gloss.en: a number of things placed on top of each other

words.example: There was a pile of clothes on the bed.

gloss.en: a small round piece of medicine to swallow

words.example: She takes a vitamin pill every morning.

gloss.en: a cloth cover for a pillow

words.example: She put a clean pillowcase on the bed.

gloss.en: a thin pointed piece of wire for fastening cloth

words.example: Use a pin to hold the cloth in place.

gloss.en: a tall evergreen tree with needle-like leaves

words.example: Tall pine trees grew on the hill.

gloss.en: a unit for measuring liquid

words.example: He drank a pint of milk.

gloss.en: the illegal copying of films, music or software

words.example: Software piracy costs companies money.

gloss.en: an area of ground marked for playing sports

words.example: The players ran onto the football pitch.

gloss.en: a feeling of sadness about something unlucky

words.example: It's a pity that you can't stay longer.

gloss.en: the act of finding someone a job or place

words.example: She found work through a job placement service.

gloss.en: a serious disease that spreads quickly and kills

words.example: The plague killed many people long ago.

gloss.en: simple and not decorated

words.example: She wore a plain but elegant dress.

gloss.en: the act of making plans for something

words.example: The team is responsible for financial planning.

gloss.en: a child that another child plays with

words.example: She was my playmate when we were young.

gloss.en: a person who writes plays

words.example: The playwright wrote a famous new drama.

gloss.en: the main events that form a story

words.example: It's hard to follow the plot of the film.

gloss.en: to pull something quickly to remove it

words.example: She tried to pluck an apple from the tree.
